knowledge_gaps: Augmentation-to-substitution ratio at 1.0-1.3:1 places AI in historically ambiguous labor zone with time-varying trajectory
Labor displacement vs augmentation rate estimates

'Hedge-destructive' Minsky category for self-funded value-destroying capex — solvent companies systematically destroying per-share value through sub-WACC investment, implying slow grind not sudden stop
AI-driven FX market microstructure transformation creates structurally fatter tails through liquidity mirages that evaporate during stress, with electronic trading at 75-80% of spot FX volume
Dollar invoicing premium from AI technology services reinforcing dollar dominance through recurring cloud subscription revenue and data gravity switching costs
AI productivity measurement bias causing monetary policy to be 50-100bp too tight due to systematic under-measurement of AI productivity gains
Rate market bimodality means long straddles on 2Y-5Y rates are structurally underpriced, with 200-300bp separation between AI-works (4.0-5.0% terminal) and AI-bust (2.0-2.5% terminal) scenarios
Dollar cycle 'plateau-then-correction' path with 55% probability and 15-25% correction over 3-5 years calibrated to 1995-2001 dot-com analogue

Equity-credit basis divergence in AI-disrupted sectors (legacy media at 6-10x equity but 120-180bp IG spreads; traditional retail similar) is exploitable with 6-9 month equity lead time from 2007-2008 precedent
EM central bank AI policy trilemma (FX stability vs. AI adoption facilitation vs. inflation credibility) as distinct from standard impossible trinity
Fed framework review (mid-2026) is an underpriced rate event, with potential 10-15bp 2Y move and 18+ months of reshaped expectations based on 2020 FAIT precedent

Stock-bond correlation regime is stage-dependent on AI productivity transmission, with Stage 1 (demand-driven) producing ambiguous correlation and Stage 2 (supply-driven) producing negative correlation
Goodwill impairment risk of $300-500B across tech acquirers from AI-related acquisitions
EM tripartite segmentation framework (supply chain beneficiaries / services-threatened / commodity-adjacent) with distinct FX factor exposures
Fed framework review (2025-2026) as under-priced front-end event risk with zero market premium for non-trivial probability of 15-30bp 2Y impact
AI narrative premium in S&P 493 non-tech stocks of 1-2 P/E turns, based on 5x increase in AI earnings call mentions and forward P/E expansion from ~16x (2022) to ~18.5x (2026)

Capex dollar as analytically distinct fourth dollar cycle driver with distinct reversal conditions (AI monetization failure, globalization of AI investment, productivity materialization reducing capex need)
focus.instruction: This topic has been studied at overview level. Focus on: (1) specific subtopics listed, (2) closing identified knowledge gaps, (3) cross-topic interactions with connected topics, (4) edge cases and regime-dependent behavior.
